subtasks.description: Retrieve all active billing contracts with contracted annual values
Retrieve all invoices issued in 2024 with amounts and associated contract IDs
Aggregate 2024 invoice amounts by contract to calculate total actual billed amount
Calculate variance between contracted annual value and actual 2024 invoiced amounts, identify contracts with >10% variance
Flag contracts with material variance (>10%) and prepare reconciliation report with details on discrepancies

plan.cost_rationale: Small tier agents handle straightforward data retrieval (SQL queries for contracts and invoices). Mid tier handles variance calculation logic and cross-reconciliation, which requires moderate complexity. Policy_action tier flags discrepancies and prepares final report since it requires judgement on materiality thresholds and remediation steps.

output.sql: SELECT c.contract_id, c.vendor_name, c.contracted_annual_value, COALESCE(SUM(i.amount_usd), 0) as actual_invoiced_2024, ROUND(100.0 * (COALESCE(SUM(i.amount_usd), 0) - COALESCE(c.contracted_annual_value, 0)) / NULLIF(c.contracted_annual_value, 0), 2) as variance_percent, CASE WHEN ABS(100.0 * (COALESCE(SUM(i.amount_usd), 0) - COALESCE(c.contracted_annual_value, 0)) / NULLIF(c.contracted_annual_value, 0)) > 10 THEN 'FLAGGED' ELSE 'OK' END as variance_flag FROM (SELECT invoice_id, contract_id, vendor_name, amount_usd, 0 as contracted_annual_value FROM invoice_facts WHERE status IN ('active', 'current') AND invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' AND invoice_date < '2025-01-01' UNION ALL SELECT NULL, contract_id, vendor_name, 0, amount_usd FROM (SELECT DISTINCT contract_id, vendor_name, amount_usd FROM invoice_facts WHERE status IN ('active', 'current')) t GROUP BY contract_id, vendor_name, amount_usd) c LEFT JOIN invoice_facts i ON c.contract_id = i.contract_id AND i.invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' AND i.invoice_date < '2025-01-01' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'active') GROUP BY c.contract_id, c.vendor_name, c.contracted_annual_value ORDER BY variance_percent DESC

SELECT i.invoice_id, i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, i.invoice_date, i.amount_usd, i.status FROM invoice_facts i WHERE i.invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' AND i.invoice_date < '2025-01-01' ORDER BY i.contract_id, i.invoice_date

output.reason: Variance analysis and reconciliation report preparation is permitted. Two contracts (C-1002: Salesforce at +24.0%, C-1004: ServiceNow at -19.44%) exceed the 10% material variance threshold and must be flagged. The upstream data for C-1006 (Okta) appears truncated/incomplete and requires human review before inclusion in the final reconciliation report. Financial reporting constraints apply: outputs must be audit-logged, flagged contracts must be escalated to contract management, and the incomplete C-1006 record must not be reported as reconciled.
applied_constraints: FLAGGED_CONTRACTS: C-1002 (Salesforce, +24.0% variance, $60,000 over-invoiced) and C-1004 (ServiceNow, -19.44% variance, $35,000 under-invoiced) must be formally flagged and escalated to contract management and finance stakeholders
INCOMPLETE_DATA_HOLD: C-1006 (Okta) upstream data is truncated; this record must be excluded from the reconciliation report and flagged as 'data_incomplete' pending re-query or manual review
AUDIT_LOG_REQUIRED: All reconciliation outputs and flagged variance decisions must be written to the audit log with timestamps, user identity, and data sources referenced
STABLE_CONTRACTS: C-1001 (Adobe, -1.25%), C-1003 (Microsoft, -1.0%), and C-1005 (Workday, +2.34%) are classified as stable and within acceptable variance thresholds; no escalation required

REPORT_DISTRIBUTION: Final reconciliation report containing contract financial data must be distributed only to authorized finance and procurement personnel; PII/vendor-sensitive fields must be access-controlled

VARIANCE_METHODOLOGY: Material variance threshold applied is >10% of contracted annual value, consistent with per-contract thresholds stored in source data (e.g., C-1002 threshold: $25,000; C-1004 threshold: $18,000)
HUMAN_REVIEW_TRIGGER: C-1006 record incompleteness must be reviewed by a human operator before the reconciliation report is marked complete or submitted to downstream systems
